proposals.statement: Warner Bros. Discovery (the target) reports three reportable segments — Streaming (HBO Max, discovery+, HBO premium pay-TV), Studios (Warner Bros. film/TV production), and Global Linear Networks (U.S. and international entertainment, lifestyle, news and sports cable networks) — having renamed its DTC segment to Streaming and its Networks segment to Global Linear Networks in Q1 2025.
proposals.quote: In the first quarter of 2025, the Company renamed its DTC reportable segment to Streaming and its Networks reportable segment to Global Linear Networks.
proposals.locator: Item 1 Business, Reportable Segments (p. 8)

proposals.statement: WBD's consolidated total revenues declined to $37,296 million in FY2025 from $39,321 million in FY2024 (down 5%), and from $41,321 million in FY2023, a multi-year revenue downtrend.
proposals.quote: 12,350 12,887 13,317 Total revenues $ 37,296 $ 39,321 $ 41,321 Revenues are attributed to each country based on the customer or viewer location.
proposals.locator: Item 7 MD&A / Note 23 Segment Reporting, three-year total revenues (p. 43)

proposals.statement: In FY2025 WBD's Streaming segment revenue grew to $12,619 million (up 9% vs FY2024's $11,607 million).
proposals.quote: Total revenues 12,619 11,607 9 %
proposals.locator: Item 7 MD&A, Streaming Segment results (p. 44)

proposals.statement: In FY2025 WBD's Studios segment revenue grew to $10,876 million (up 5% vs FY2024's $10,313 million).
proposals.quote: Total revenues 10,876 10,313 5 %
proposals.locator: Item 7 MD&A, Studios Segment results (p. 45)

proposals.statement: In FY2025 WBD's Global Linear Networks segment revenue fell to $17,656 million (down 12% vs FY2024's $20,175 million) — the largest segment but in decline.
proposals.quote: Total revenues 17,656 20,175 (12) %
proposals.locator: Item 7 MD&A, Global Linear Networks Segment results (p. 46)

proposals.statement: WBD's FY2025 segment revenue mix is approximately: Global Linear Networks $17,656M (~47% of the $37,296M total), Streaming $12,619M (~34%), and Studios $10,876M (~29%), before inter-segment eliminations — i.e. the declining linear-networks business is still the single largest revenue contributor while the growing Streaming and Studios segments together are the majority.
WBD's total company Adjusted EBITDA was $8,744 million in FY2025, down 3% from $9,032 million in FY2024.
proposals.quote: Other inter-segment eliminations (68) 16 NM NM Adjusted EBITDA $ 8,744 $ 9,032 (3) %
proposals.locator: Item 7 MD&A, Adjusted EBITDA by segment (p. 43)

proposals.statement: WBD's Global Linear Networks segment generated $6,412 million of Adjusted EBITDA in FY2025, down 21% year-over-year.
proposals.quote: Adjusted EBITDA - Global Linear Networks segment 6,412 8,149 (21) %
proposals.locator: Item 7 MD&A, Global Linear Networks Segment (p. 46)

proposals.statement: WBD's Studios segment Adjusted EBITDA rose 54% to $2,545 million in FY2025 (from $1,652 million), and its Streaming segment Adjusted EBITDA increased $693 million in 2025.
proposals.quote: Adjusted EBITDA - Studios segment 2,545 1,652 54 %
proposals.locator: Item 7 MD&A, Studios Segment (p. 45)

proposals.statement: WBD's combined Streaming & Studios division generated revenues of $20,371 million in FY2025 (up 3%) and Adjusted EBITDA of $3,496 million (up 64% from $2,127 million) — the segment grouping that was to become the Warner Bros. company in the planned separation.
proposals.quote: Streaming & Studios 20,371 19,791 3 %
proposals.locator: Item 7 MD&A, Supplemental Streaming & Studios and Global Linear Networks Division Information (p. 43)

proposals.statement: WBD's Streaming & Studios division Adjusted EBITDA grew 64% to $3,496 million in FY2025 (from $2,127 million).
proposals.quote: Streaming & Studios 3,496 2,127 64 %
proposals.locator: Item 7 MD&A, Supplemental Division Adjusted EBITDA (p. 43)

proposals.statement: On the eve of the deal the declining Global Linear Networks segment still produced roughly 1.8x the Adjusted EBITDA of the growing Streaming & Studios division ($6,412M / $3,496M ≈ 1.83x) — so Global Linear Networks supplied the majority of WBD's segment-level cash earnings even though its EBITDA fell 21% while Streaming & Studios' rose 64%; this frames the q_whole_co_vs_jewels question, since the 'melting' linear asset is the larger near-term cash generator while the crown-jewel studios/streaming division is the growth engine.

Before the Paramount deal, WBD had a board-approved plan to separate into two companies — a Streaming & Studios business and a Global Linear Networks business — and Paramount's obligation to close is expressly conditioned on WBD NOT having completed that separation or paid a separation dividend.
proposals.quote: In addition, PSKY’s obligation to consummate the PSKY Merger is subject to WBD not having completed the separation of its Streaming & Studios business from its Global Linear Networks business nor having declared or made any dividend to WBD’s stockholders to effectuate the separation.
proposals.locator: Item 1A Risk Factors, PSKY Merger conditions (separation of Streaming & Studios from Global Linear Networks)

proposals.statement: WBD reported approximately 35,500 employees as of December 31, 2025 (48% in the U.S., 52% outside the U.S.).
proposals.quote: As of December 31, 2025, we had approximately 35,500 employees, including full-time and part-time employees of our wholly-owned subsidiaries and consolidated ventures, with 48% located in the U.S. and 52% located outside of the U.S.
proposals.locator: Item 1 Business, Human Capital (p. 12)

proposals.statement: WBD ended 2025 with 131.6 million Streaming subscribers, having added 14.7 million global subscribers during 2025 as HBO Max expanded to over 100 markets.
proposals.quote: As of December 31, 2025, we had 131.6 million Streaming subscribers
proposals.locator: Item 1 Business, Streaming segment (p. 8)

proposals.statement: WBD's stated strategy is to grow streaming globally, enhance Studios, and manage the linear networks for the best possible success — explicitly treating linear as a business to be 'managed' rather than grown.
proposals.quote: Our asset mix strongly positions us to execute our key strategies: grow our streaming business globally, enhance our Studios segment, and manage our linear networks for the best possible success in order to create long-term value for our shareholders.
proposals.locator: Item 1 Business, strategy overview (p. 8)

proposals.statement: WBD's distribution/advertising/content revenue mix differs sharply by segment: Streaming is 87% distribution / 9% advertising / 4% content; Studios is 93% content / 7% other; and Global Linear Networks is 55% distribution / 36% advertising / 7% content / 2% other — making the linear segment the company's main advertising-revenue exposure.
proposals.quote: For the year ended December 31, 2025, distribution, advertising, content, and other revenues were 55%, 36%, 7%, and 2%, respectively, of total revenues for this segment.
proposals.locator: Item 1 Business, Global Linear Networks segment (p. 9)

proposals.statement: In 2025 S&P, Moody's and Fitch downgraded certain WBD ratings, citing declines in its linear business, the weak operating environment, its leverage ratio, an increase in secured debt, and uncertainty over the previously planned separation — evidence of credit-quality erosion at the target.
proposals.quote: In 2025, S&P, Moody’s and Fitch downgraded certain of our ratings in part due to declines in our linear business, including as a result of the weak operating environment, our leverage ratio, and an increase in secured debt and uncertainty in connection with the previously planned separation of Warner Bros.
proposals.locator: Item 1A Risk Factors, credit ratings

proposals.statement: WBD paid $2,295 million of cash interest on its outstanding debt in 2025 (up from $1,996 million in 2024) and $1,926 million of cash income taxes — sizing the target's standalone debt-service burden before the Paramount leveraged acquisition.
proposals.quote: During 2025 and 2024, we made cash payments of $1,926 million and $1,113 million for income taxes and $2,295 million and $1,996 million for interest on our outstanding debt, respectively.
proposals.locator: Item 7 MD&A, Liquidity and Capital Resources / interest paid

proposals.statement: The acquirer, Paramount Skydance, operates three reportable segments — TV Media (CBS broadcast network and cable networks), Direct-to-Consumer (Paramount+, Pluto TV), and Filmed Entertainment (Paramount Pictures) — and was transitioning to Studios / Direct-to-Consumer / TV Media reporting from Q1 2026.
proposals.quote: The Company is comprised of three segments: TV Media , Direct-to-Consumer , and Filmed Entertainment (see Note 13).
proposals.locator: Q3 2025 Form 10-Q, Notes, Segment Information (Note 13)

proposals.statement: Paramount Skydance forecast full-year 2025 total revenue of approximately $30 billion (with Q3 2025 quarterly revenue of $6,731 million and Q4 2025 revenue guided to $8.1-$8.3 billion), and guided to 2026 total revenue of $30 billion (4% growth).

source.title: Paramount Skydance Q3 2025 earnings release (8-K Ex-99)

proposals.quote: For 2026, we expect total revenue of $30 billion, or 4% growth year-over-year versus the midpoint of our 2025 forecast, led by a healthy acceleration in DTC revenue.
proposals.locator: Q3 2025 earnings release (8-K Ex-99), 2026 outlook

proposals.statement: Paramount Skydance's TV Media revenue declined 12% year-over-year in Q3 2025, while its Direct-to-Consumer (streaming) revenue grew 17%, driven by a 24% increase in Paramount+ revenue (which is over 80% of the DTC business) — the same linear-decline / streaming-growth split the target exhibits.

source.title: Paramount Skydance Q3 2025 earnings release (8-K Ex-99)

proposals.quote: Revenue in our DTC business increased by 17% year-over-year, driven by a 24% increase in Paramount+ revenue, which accounts for over 80% of our DTC business.
proposals.locator: Q3 2025 earnings release (8-K Ex-99), segment revenue breakdown (p. 9)

proposals.statement: Paramount+ reached 79.1 million global subscribers at the end of Q3 2025, up 13% year-over-year (from 71.9 million), with subscribers and ARPU each contributing roughly equally (10% and 11% growth) to revenue.

source.title: Paramount Skydance Q3 2025 earnings release (8-K Ex-99)

proposals.quote: Paramount+ Subscribers 71.9 77.5 79.0 77.7 79.1 Y/Y Growth (%) 13%
proposals.locator: Q3 2025 earnings release (8-K Ex-99), Paramount+ subscriber trend table

proposals.statement: Paramount Skydance reported its Direct-to-Consumer segment turning to positive Adjusted OIBDA and raised its run-rate cost-efficiency target from $2 billion to at least $3 billion, expecting to complete its transformation program by the end of 2027.

source.title: Paramount Skydance Q3 2025 earnings release (8-K Ex-99)

proposals.quote: OIBDA 1 of $3.5 billion and are increasing our run-rate efficiency target from $2 billion to at least $3 billion.
proposals.locator: Q3 2025 earnings release (8-K Ex-99), efficiency target

proposals.statement: Paramount Skydance's stated strategy (management's own words) is to transform Paramount into 'the global home of world-class storytelling,' powered by its studio, the leading broadcast network (CBS), and a global scaled streaming platform — an explicit scale-the-streamer thesis.

source.title: Paramount Skydance Q3 2025 earnings release (8-K Ex-99)

proposals.quote: Our vision is to transform Paramount into the global home of world-class storytelling, powered by one of the industry’s most storied studios, the leading broadcast network, and a global, scaled streaming platform that delivers must-watch programming to audiences everywhere.
proposals.locator: Q3 2025 earnings release (8-K Ex-99), CEO letter / vision

proposals.statement: Paramount+ revenue grew 25% year-over-year in Q3 2025 ($1,771 million in Q3'25), and Paramount Skydance expects its DTC business to be profitable on a full-year basis in 2025 — an interested-party (management) profitability assertion.

source.title: Paramount Skydance Q3 2025 earnings release (8-K Ex-99)

proposals.quote: On a full-year basis, we expect DTC to be profitable in 2025 with growth in profitability in 2026.
proposals.locator: Q3 2025 earnings release (8-K Ex-99), DTC outlook

proposals.statement: The acquirer (Paramount Skydance, ~$30B revenue) and target (WBD, $37.3B revenue) are structurally near-mirror media companies — each with a declining linear/TV-networks business, a growing-but-subscale streaming service (Paramount+ ~79M subs vs HBO Max-led ~131.6M WBD streaming subs), and a film/TV studio — so the combination roughly doubles revenue scale and stacks two sub-scale streamers, consistent with Paramount's stated 'global, scaled streaming platform' strategy.
